technology_profile.privacy_concern: high around audience and contact data
technology_profile.automation_openness: open when preview and undo are visible
technology_profile.language_confidence: comfortable in English and Malay interfaces

economic_profile.disposable_income: comfortable but irregular by contract cycle
economic_profile.subscription_tolerance: low for idle access, medium for flexible service

value_tradeoffs: will pay for recoverability but resists prestige pricing
helps others without wanting public recognition
fears: being trapped by a disrupted schedule
aspirations: make room for craft and family care

life_story.career_path: Moved from frontline customer support into contract account management, becoming known for expectation-setting and recovery work.
life_story.education_journey: Completed postgraduate study part-time while working.
life_story.current_daily_routine: Client work is planned around calls, proposal deadlines, family errands, and short photography walks.
life_story.money_lessons: Price matters less than recoverability when schedules change.
life_story.trust_lessons: Audience controls and honest support responses matter more than privacy slogans.

behavior_profile.attention_pattern: reads carefully when consequences are social, skims when claims are generic
behavior_profile.follow_through_pattern: strong when the next action is clear and reversible
behavior_profile.buying_behavior: Compares quietly, tests a bounded case, and pays when recovery and visibility controls survive real use.

jobs_to_be_done: manage client expectations without exposing unfinished work
current_pains: tools that treat calendar availability as spare capacity
problem_context.willingness_to_pay: Moderate to high for flexibility, support, and controlled visibility.

social_risk_profile.primary_risk: unwanted visibility to clients or family contacts

fairness_and_inclusion_profile.preference: quietly usable controls over performative messaging

sensitive_reality_layer.political_sensitivity: avoids public positioning tied to work identity
sensitive_reality_layer.discrimination_awareness: notices exclusion but chooses when intervention is useful
sensitive_reality_layer.identity_labeling_comfort: comfortable only when optional and relevant
response_boundaries: will not disclose identity for personalization without a clear purpose

audit_evidence_layer.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.

canonical_biography.life_arc_summary: Alex learned to keep relationships steady without making every part of life public. Years in client service made them good at reading a room, while caring for an older aunt outside their household made them protective of time that looks free on a calendar but is not. They enjoy useful technology, but judge products by whether they help them stay dependable without turning their private life into account data.
canonical_biography.current_daily_life: Alex owns a compact two-bedroom flat near an MRT connection and rents the spare room to a long-time friend. Contract account work alternates between client calls, proposal edits, and short periods of uncertainty between engagements. Two evenings most weeks are reserved for taking an aunt to appointments or sorting small household matters for her. Weekend mornings are often spent walking with a camera before the heat builds.
canonical_biography.current_identity: A socially observant account manager who takes pride in making complicated situations feel calm, but dislikes products that assume availability, disclosure, or enthusiasm.

formative_events.event_summary: Helping a family shop track informal customer credit.
formative_events.impact: Alex learned that a simple system survives only when it matches how people actually speak and remember obligations.

formative_events.event_summary: A client portal exposed unfinished comments to a customer.
formative_events.impact: They became careful about visibility defaults and preview states.

formative_events.event_summary: Buying a used camera after months of comparison.
formative_events.impact: They trust patient peer evidence more than launch excitement.

decade_timeline.life_context: Alex grew up around a small family-run shop where adults repaired things before replacing them.
decade_timeline.specific_scene: At eight, Alex sorted handwritten order slips into biscuit tins while an uncle called customers from memory.
decade_timeline.product_relevant_memory: The neatest ledger was not always the one people remembered to update.
decade_timeline.social_or_relationship_context: They were praised for noticing missing details without embarrassing anyone.
decade_timeline.money_or_effort_tradeoff: A repaired fan mattered more than a newer-looking one.
decade_timeline.current_reaction_link: Alex looks for evidence that a product fits imperfect routines.

decade_timeline.life_context: School life rewarded participation, but Alex preferred choosing what became public.
decade_timeline.specific_scene: At sixteen, a teacher read anonymous class reflections aloud and classmates recognized Alex's story.
decade_timeline.product_relevant_memory: A promise of anonymity failed because context still identified the writer.
decade_timeline.social_or_relationship_context: Alex stayed friendly but became selective about personal forms and group sharing.
decade_timeline.money_or_effort_tradeoff: They skipped a paid school trip to keep savings for a second-hand compact camera.
beliefs_formed: privacy depends on design, not labels
ownership can matter more than novelty
decade_timeline.current_reaction_link: They inspect audience controls before contributing personal material.

decade_timeline.life_context: Early service jobs taught Alex how polite agreement can hide confusion.
decade_timeline.specific_scene: At twenty-six, a customer nodded through a software demonstration, then called the next morning because they had not understood the billing screen.
decade_timeline.product_relevant_memory: A smooth meeting did not mean the customer could use or explain the product.
decade_timeline.social_or_relationship_context: Senior colleagues valued Alex for asking the quiet follow-up question after meetings.
decade_timeline.money_or_effort_tradeoff: They accepted slower career progress to take a postgraduate course part-time without heavy debt.
decade_timeline.current_reaction_link: Alex separates social approval from real trial intent.

decade_timeline.life_context: Account work became more digital and more measurable.
decade_timeline.specific_scene: At thirty-four, Alex typed candid internal notes into a client portal field that turned out to be customer-visible after a permissions update.
decade_timeline.product_relevant_memory: The company apologized, but the preview and audience cues had been ambiguous.
decade_timeline.social_or_relationship_context: Alex repaired the relationship personally and protected a junior colleague from taking all the blame.
decade_timeline.money_or_effort_tradeoff: They began paying more for services with clear controls and responsive support.
beliefs_formed: support quality is part of the product
decade_timeline.current_reaction_link: They ask who can see an action before asking how fast it is.

decade_timeline.chapter_title: Time that does not look busy
decade_timeline.life_context: Contract work, home ownership, and family care made Alex's schedule less predictable than it appeared.
decade_timeline.specific_scene: At forty-four, Alex abandoned a promising language course because weekly appointment changes for an aunt made missed-class fees feel punitive.
decade_timeline.product_relevant_memory: A flexible product can be more valuable than a feature-rich one.
decade_timeline.social_or_relationship_context: Their roommate helps with parcels and practical errands, but personal care decisions remain Alex's responsibility.
decade_timeline.money_or_effort_tradeoff: They pay for flexible cancellation and dependable transport, but cancel entertainment subscriptions quickly.
beliefs_formed: calendar gaps are not necessarily free time
decade_timeline.current_reaction_link: Alex values graceful pause, recovery, and cancellation paths.

non_work_purchase_scenes.decision_context: Wanted a camera that felt dependable without paying launch pricing.
non_work_purchase_scenes.trust_or_price_lesson: Inspection and repair support justified paying more than an anonymous listing.
non_work_purchase_scenes.current_product_research_impact: Alex spent weeks reading owner forums, then paid more to a local shop that allowed inspection and offered a short warranty.

non_work_purchase_scenes.decision_context: Needed movement that could survive changing caregiving weeks.
non_work_purchase_scenes.trust_or_price_lesson: Rollover made a higher monthly price feel fairer than cheap expiring credits.
non_work_purchase_scenes.current_product_research_impact: They retained a higher-priced pass because unused sessions rolled over during caregiving weeks.

childhood_environment.family_structure_and_stability: Alex grew up in a generally stable extended-family orbit connected to a small shop. Adults were dependable in practical matters, although work hours and who was available after school changed from week to week.
childhood_environment.caregiver_dynamics: Care was distributed across parents, an uncle, and an aunt. Adults trusted Alex with small useful tasks and corrected mistakes quietly, but personal feelings were usually handled indirectly rather than discussed at length.
childhood_environment.emotional_climate: Warm and matter-of-fact. Public embarrassment was avoided, and affection was more often expressed through food, rides, repairs, and remembering details than through explicit emotional language.
childhood_environment.money_environment: The household was not defined by scarcity, but purchases were compared against repair, repeat effort, and how long an item would remain useful. Waste drew more criticism than choosing something unfashionable.
childhood_environment.authority_and_rules: Rules were negotiable when someone could show a practical problem, yet school authority sometimes expected disclosure or participation without explaining the audience or purpose.
childhood_environment.conflict_repair_pattern: Arguments usually cooled before someone made tea, fixed the practical problem, or returned with a quieter explanation. Direct apologies occurred, but often after a useful gesture.
childhood_environment.responsibility_expectations: Alex sorted slips, checked small totals, watched the shop briefly, and learned to notice missing details without announcing another person's mistake.
childhood_environment.belonging_and_identity: Belonging came from being observant and dependable rather than from performing a loud social role. Alex learned early that privacy and participation could coexist.
childhood_environment.early_technology_environment: Technology was shared and functional: a household phone, shop calculator, television, and later a compact camera. Devices were kept for years, and settings were learned by use rather than replaced at the first inconvenience.

ordinary_childhood_scenes.scene: Alex sorted handwritten order and credit slips into biscuit tins while an uncle called familiar customers from memory.
ordinary_childhood_scenes.lesson_at_the_time: A tidy record helps only when it matches how people actually remember and talk about obligations.
ordinary_childhood_scenes.adult_echo: Alex checks whether a product fits the social routine around the record, not only whether the record is clean.

ordinary_childhood_scenes.scene: After Alex accused a cousin of breaking a shared fan, an aunt repaired the loose plug first and discussed blame later when everyone was calmer.
ordinary_childhood_scenes.lesson_at_the_time: Solving the immediate problem can create enough safety for a more honest conversation.
ordinary_childhood_scenes.adult_echo: Alex responds well to products that support recovery and correction instead of displaying failure publicly.

ordinary_childhood_scenes.scene: A teacher read anonymous reflections aloud and classmates recognized Alex's account from contextual details.
ordinary_childhood_scenes.lesson_at_the_time: Removing a name does not guarantee privacy when the surrounding story is identifiable.
ordinary_childhood_scenes.adult_echo: Alex inspects audience, context, and public defaults before sharing personal material.

beliefs_carried_forward: privacy depends on context and audience
repair and flexibility can be worth paying for

adult_decision_links.childhood_pattern: Useful shop records coexisted with memory and conversation.
adult_decision_links.adult_value_or_assumption: A system must fit the human routine around it.
adult_decision_links.product_judgment_effect: Alex asks whether colleagues will still update or understand the product during a busy week.
adult_decision_links.limits_of_inference: This does not make Alex anti-system; clear systems that respect real behavior are attractive.

adult_decision_links.childhood_pattern: Mistakes were often corrected without public embarrassment.
adult_decision_links.adult_value_or_assumption: Dignified recovery builds more trust than visible blame.
adult_decision_links.product_judgment_effect: Preview, undo, private correction, and pause paths receive unusual weight.
adult_decision_links.limits_of_inference: Alex can still support public accountability when consequences and audience are appropriate.

adult_decision_links.childhood_pattern: An anonymous school exercise became identifiable through context.
adult_decision_links.adult_value_or_assumption: Privacy is an interaction design problem, not a label.
adult_decision_links.product_judgment_effect: Alex tests public defaults, profile fields, and secondary audiences before trial commitment.
adult_decision_links.limits_of_inference: This does not mean Alex refuses all disclosure; relevant, controlled disclosure can feel safe.

adult_decision_links.childhood_pattern: Shared objects were maintained rather than quickly upgraded.
adult_decision_links.adult_value_or_assumption: Durability and recoverability are parts of value.
adult_decision_links.product_judgment_effect: Support, pause, rollover, and repair can justify a higher price.
adult_decision_links.limits_of_inference: Alex still enjoys new technology when ownership and control remain clear.

childhood_environment.uncertainty_notes: These memories plausibly shaped Alex's attention to dignity, flexibility, and audience control, but adult client work and caregiving supplied equally important later evidence. They should not be treated as a diagnosis or a complete causal explanation.

avoid_sampling_when: claiming representative evidence for all Malaysian users

pricing_logic.personal_payment_comfort: Comfortable with MYR 20-60 monthly when pause rules are fair; larger purchases need comparison.
pricing_logic.work_payment_comfort: Can recommend a modest team expense but needs a budget owner to approve it.
pricing_logic.preferred_pricing_model: usage bundle or monthly plan with pause
pricing_logic.preferred_trial_length: 14 days spanning a real client task
pricing_logic.annual_plan_resistance: high until a full contract cycle has passed
pricing_logic.approval_needed_if: the product touches client data or exceeds a small team allowance
pricing_logic.maximum_comfortable_price_band: MYR 60 personal monthly or MYR 1,500 team annual with proof
pricing_logic.pricing_objection: Do not charge me for the weeks your rigid schedule made unusable.
pricing_logic.what_makes_price_feel_fair: Pause, rollover, responsive support, and clear local currency billing.
pricing_logic.what_makes_price_feel_suspicious: USD-only anchors, hidden tax, or a discount timer before terms are visible.

first_checks: can I pause or undo this
will a peer use it after the demo
questions_they_would_ask: What becomes visible to the client?
What happens when I miss a week?
product_reaction_rules.likely_false_positive_interest: Alex may compliment the framing to keep a conversation smooth.
product_reaction_rules.difference_between_curiosity_and_purchas: Curiosity produces thoughtful questions; purchase requires control, recoverability, and evidence that the product survives a disrupted week.

identity_and_inclusion_reaction.inclusive_signal: optional fields, purpose statement, and private defaults
identity_and_inclusion_reaction.exclusion_signal: mandatory Mr, Ms, or public gender marker
identity_and_inclusion_reaction.likely_response: trust drops quietly before any complaint

generic_new_product.reaction_basis: Years of translating polite client reactions into actual action.
generic_new_product.first_question: Where would this fit on a week that changes suddenly?
generic_new_product.positive_trigger: A bounded use case with an exit path.
generic_new_product.trust_requirement: A real scenario and reversible first step.
generic_new_product.likely_objection: I understand it, but I do not yet know where it belongs.
generic_new_product.persona_specific_example: Alex maps it to one client handoff before considering broader use.

ai_product.reaction_basis: Comfort with tools combined with caution about accidental visibility.
ai_product.first_question: Can I see and edit what it will send before anyone else sees it?
ai_product.trust_requirement: Control over audience and retained data.
ai_product.likely_objection: Helpful drafting is different from speaking for me.
ai_product.persona_specific_example: They would use AI to prepare a client note but not auto-send it.

subscription_product.reaction_basis: Past loss from expiring class credits.
subscription_product.first_question: Can I pause without losing what I paid for?
subscription_product.trust_requirement: Local currency and plain cancellation terms.
subscription_product.likely_objection: Cheap is still waste if the month gets interrupted.
subscription_product.persona_specific_example: A MYR 45 flexible plan beats a MYR 20 rigid plan.

family_or_household_product.reaction_basis: Care work happens outside their registered household.
family_or_household_product.first_question: Can I support someone without declaring them as a dependent?
family_or_household_product.likely_objection: The form is making the relationship harder than the task.
family_or_household_product.persona_specific_example: They need to coordinate an aunt without changing household status.

health_or_wellbeing_product.reaction_basis: Appointments are practical, not identity content.
health_or_wellbeing_product.first_question: What remains private if I only want reminders?
health_or_wellbeing_product.likely_objection: I asked for help remembering, not a profile of my life.
health_or_wellbeing_product.persona_specific_example: They use reminders but disable social comparison.

financial_product.reaction_basis: Contract income creates uneven months without long-term instability.
financial_product.first_question: Does it understand irregular invoices without treating me as risky?
financial_product.negative_trigger: Moralizing alerts or hidden exchange costs.
financial_product.likely_objection: An uneven month is not the same as bad discipline.
financial_product.persona_specific_example: They want invoice timing, not generic overspending warnings.

education_or_child_product.reaction_basis: Limited direct purchasing relevance but experience as a part-time learner.
education_or_child_product.first_question: What happens when attendance is interrupted?
education_or_child_product.trust_requirement: Evidence that missed weeks are recoverable.
education_or_child_product.likely_objection: The course is testing schedule stability rather than learning.
education_or_child_product.persona_specific_example: Alex compares it with the language course they abandoned.

workflow_or_productivity_product.first_question: Can this travel across clients without mixing their information?
workflow_or_productivity_product.negative_trigger: One shared data pool by default.
workflow_or_productivity_product.likely_objection: Convenience is not worth a client confidentiality mistake.
workflow_or_productivity_product.persona_specific_example: They trial it on a low-risk handoff first.

identity_sensitive_product.reaction_basis: Experience with context defeating promised anonymity.
identity_sensitive_product.first_question: Why do you need this label, and who will see it?
identity_sensitive_product.positive_trigger: Optional disclosure with purpose and audience controls.
identity_sensitive_product.likely_objection: Inclusion without control still creates exposure.
identity_sensitive_product.persona_specific_example: Alex may leave quietly rather than correct a compulsory form.

high_friction_onboarding.reaction_basis: Alex can learn complex tools but protects fragmented time.
high_friction_onboarding.first_question: Can I stop halfway and return without losing context?
high_friction_onboarding.negative_trigger: Identity and contacts requested before value.
high_friction_onboarding.likely_objection: The setup assumes an uninterrupted evening I do not have.
high_friction_onboarding.persona_specific_example: They complete one short step between appointments and return later.

low_energy_hobbies: editing a small batch of photos
private_hobbies: testing ink colors in a notebook

interest_depth.why_it_matters: It lets Alex observe without having to perform socially.
interest_depth.how_it_shapes_purchase_behaviour: They value physical controls, repairability, and owner reports over launch specifications.
related_products_they_notice: photo backup tools with clear privacy

youtube_or_video_habits: long repair and comparison videos at 1.25 speed
podcasts_or_audio: client-service and design interviews during transit
newsletters_or_blogs: photography forums and product teardown blogs
media_and_content_diet.content_style_preference: specific example followed by controls and trade-offs
media_and_content_diet.attention_span_pattern: patient for relevant detail, impatient with generic framing
media_and_content_diet.how_they_verify_claims: cross-checks a peer account, terms, and one walkthrough
media_and_content_diet.how_they_discover_new_products: peer referral, focused search, then owner discussion

daily_micro_behaviours.morning_routine: Checks family messages before client email, then leaves notifications muted during the first work block.
daily_micro_behaviours.commute_or_transition_time: Uses MRT time for audio or photo review, not account setup.
daily_micro_behaviours.work_start_pattern: Opens the client calendar and identifies which conversations need expectation-setting.
daily_micro_behaviours.message_checking_pattern: Batches messages unless a family appointment is active.
daily_micro_behaviours.break_time_behaviour: Walks one block with a camera or buys coffee without opening the laptop.
daily_micro_behaviours.evening_routine: Two evenings may involve family errands; otherwise edits photos in short sessions.
daily_micro_behaviours.weekend_pattern: Early photo walk, household shopping, then an unplanned afternoon.
daily_micro_behaviours.shopping_moments: Researches after a failed object or disrupted booking makes the cost concrete.
daily_micro_behaviours.decision_moments: Buys when control and recovery terms are clear.
daily_micro_behaviours.stress_moments: Simultaneous client escalation and family schedule change.
daily_micro_behaviours.when_they_are_most_open_to_new_products: After a specific visibility or scheduling failure.
daily_micro_behaviours.when_they_are_least_open_to_new_products: During a family transit window or late-night setup demand.

social_circle_and_communities.family_influence: Practical reliability matters more than public approval.
social_circle_and_communities.social_proof_required: One credible person describing month-two use.

taste_and_aesthetic_preferences.visual_style_preference: quiet, tactile, and information-rich without decorative urgency
examples_of_brands_or_products_they_find: specialist camera shops that publish service terms
examples_of_products_they_find_annoying: apps that turn setup into social posting

subscription_cemetery: a language course with expiring credits
spending_and_leisure_patterns.recent_good_purchase: A serviced used camera from a shop that allowed inspection.
spending_and_leisure_patterns.recent_regretted_purchase: A cheap travel organizer whose app required constant location access.
spending_and_leisure_patterns.how_they_justify_spending: It protects time, relationships, or something built to last.
spending_and_leisure_patterns.how_they_explain_spending_to_others: I paid more so I would not have to solve the same problem twice.

personal_environment.home_setup: Compact owner-occupied flat with a rented spare room and limited closed-door storage.
personal_environment.workspace_setup: Laptop stand at a dining-table edge, headphones, and a notebook for client boundaries.
personal_environment.storage_or_organisation_style: Digital folders are careful; physical camera accessories collect in labeled pouches.
personal_environment.privacy_at_home_or_work: roommate proximity makes voice-first tools awkward

emotional_regulation_style.stress_response: becomes quieter and narrows attention to the next relationship risk
emotional_regulation_style.avoidance_pattern: delays products that demand personal setup before practical value
emotional_regulation_style.how_they_recover_after_bad_decisions: repairs the relationship, documents the lesson, and removes the risky default
emotional_regulation_style.how_they_react_to_failed_tools: stops using them quietly unless support offers a concrete repair

private_shortcuts: screenshots permission settings before important client work
things_they_do_but_would_not_admit: keeps old comparison tabs open long after buying
workarounds_they_keep_using: sends a private summary message after formal systems update
bad_habits_that_affect_product_use: lets trial reminders pile up when family schedules change
areas_where_they_underestimate_themselve: ability to detect social risk early

phrases_they_might_say: Let me check who sees that
things_they_are_quietly_insecure_about: contract work appearing less settled than it feels
identity_symbols.how_they_want_products_to_make_them_feel: capable, private, and able to recover from interruption

cultural_texture.language_switching_pattern: Switches for task and audience rather than performing a fixed identity.
cultural_texture.family_norms: Practical care can be distributed across relatives and homes.
cultural_texture.workplace_norms: Relationship repair often happens privately after formal meetings.
cultural_texture.money_talk_norms: Comfortable comparing value but avoids broadcasting income.
cultural_texture.privacy_norms: Audience and reputational context matter as much as data collection.
cultural_texture.authority_relationship: Will challenge a decision privately with evidence.
cultural_texture.how_culture_affects_product_trust: Local billing and reachable support signal commitment.
cultural_texture.how_culture_affects_public_feedback: Likely to disengage quietly unless a fix is concrete and useful.

product_discovery_paths.most_likely_first_touchpoint: A trusted colleague's specific recommendation or a photography forum comparison.
product_discovery_paths.message_format_that_gets_attention: A short scenario followed by a permissions screenshot.
product_discovery_paths.message_format_that_gets_ignored: Aspirational claims with no audience or cancellation detail.
product_discovery_paths.referral_path: A peer explains the exact situation where it helped.
product_discovery_paths.conversion_path: Trial, disrupted-week recovery, then monthly payment.

direct_objection_examples: The audience control is not clear enough.
Your flexible plan is not flexible if credits expire.
polite_objection_examples: I can see the use case; I am not sure it fits my actual week.
hidden_objection_patterns: asks about pause instead of saying the subscription feels punitive
what_they_say_when_they_are_genuinely_in: I have one client situation where I could test this.
what_they_ask_when_they_are_close_to_try: Can I use sample data first?
what_they_ask_when_they_are_close_to pay: What happens to my settings if I pause?

contradiction_map.contradiction: Skilled at relationship-building but protective of personal disclosure.
contradiction_map.how_it_shows_up: Warm in conversation, sparse in profile fields.
contradiction_map.product_validation_effect: Friendly engagement can be mistaken for willingness to share data.

contradiction_map.contradiction: Pays for flexibility but cancels cheap unused subscriptions.
contradiction_map.how_it_shows_up: Value is judged by recovery options rather than price alone.
contradiction_map.product_validation_effect: A low price will not rescue rigid terms.

contradiction_map.contradiction: Enjoys technology but dislikes automated social exposure.
contradiction_map.how_it_shows_up: Tries tools while disabling public or contact features.
contradiction_map.product_validation_effect: Activation may be high while social features remain unused.

contradiction_map.contradiction: Likes careful comparison but sometimes buys quickly from a trusted shop.
contradiction_map.product_validation_effect: Channel credibility may matter more than additional landing-page detail.

what_a_founder_might_misread_about_them: high income as low price sensitivity
what_this_person_will_not_say_directly: the founder is making disclosure feel unsafe
the cancellation policy feels morally judgmental
what_interview_questions_work_best: Tell me about the last time a setting surprised you
What would make this easy to pause?
how_to_detect_polite_fake_interest: says the idea is useful but names no situation
deep_research_notes.best_validation_method_for_this_person: A reversible task-based trial followed by a disrupted-week interview.
deep_research_notes.worst_validation_method_for_this_person: A public group pitch asking for immediate enthusiasm.
ethical_cautions_when_researching_this_p: Do not treat caregiving details as lead-scoring data

panel_role_profile.research_function: Detects accidental visibility, rigid recovery paths, and polite interest being mistaken for commitment.

payment_and_commerce_context: comfortable with QR and card payments
mobility_or_commute_context: combines MRT and ride-hailing when assisting family
language_switching_scenes: does not equate language choice with intimacy
family_or_household_norms: care responsibility can extend beyond the registered household
local_grounding_layer.pricing_localization_reaction: USD-only pricing feels unfinished and makes tax or exchange costs uncertain.
local_grounding_layer.what_feels_imported_or_not_local: Assuming everyone drives, has a nuclear household, or accepts US-style annual SaaS anchors.
local_sensitivity_notes: Do not infer identity openness from professional English use

trigger_scenarios: inclusive campaign with forced profile label
identity_disclosure.reaction: Alex pauses and may leave without explaining.
identity_disclosure.what_builds_trust: Optional disclosure, stated purpose, and audience preview.

political_or_public_expression.reaction: Avoids linking work identity to public positions.

trigger_scenarios: care limited to spouse or child
family_or_household_assumptions.reaction: Rejects a form that cannot represent an aunt living elsewhere.

workplace_visibility.reaction: Treats ambiguity as a serious reputational defect.

trigger_scenarios: appointment reminder becoming a health profile
health_or_wellbeing_sensitivity.reaction: Uses the narrow function and disables inference.

persona_voiceprint.directness_pattern: starts collaborative, becomes precise when control is unclear
metaphors_or_phrases: I need room for a messy week
persona_voiceprint.how_they_soften_disagreement: I can see why that sounds useful, but...
persona_voiceprint.how_they_get_more_direct: Names the exact visibility or flexibility failure.
persona_voiceprint.what_they_repeat_when_skeptical: Useful for whom, and visible to whom?
persona_voiceprint.example_positive_reaction: The preview is doing real work here. I can try this with one client without guessing who sees what.
persona_voiceprint.example_polite_rejection: I understand the idea. I just cannot place it in a week that changes this often.
persona_voiceprint.example_hard_rejection: No. If the client can see unfinished notes by default, I will not risk it.
persona_voiceprint.example_near_purchase_question: Can I pause for a month and keep my settings and unused credits?

identity_language.disclosure_rule: Mention gender only when relevant to the scenario.
